description_long: This apartment building features a simple rectangular structure with a steep, reddish-brown gabled roof and a small chimney on one side. The facade is gray with two horizontal bands separating three distinct floors, each lined with symmetrically arranged arched windows. The front side includes a single arched doorway at the center of the ground floor, flanked by evenly spaced windows. The roof overhangs slightly, providing a modest eave, and the building lacks balconies or decorative elements. The rear and side views show the same window pattern, with the rear lacking an entrance. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on symmetry and function, typical of traditional European residential architecture.
description: A three-story, gray apartment building with a reddish-brown gabled roof, arched windows, and a central arched entrance. The design is symmetrical and functional, with no balconies or ornate features.
description_view_0: The narrow gable end of the building is visible, showing the steep roof and the vertical alignment of windows on three floors.
description_view_1: The front facade displays the main entrance, three rows of arched windows, and the chimney on the right side of the roof.
description_view_2: The opposite gable end is shown, highlighting the roof's pitch and the symmetrical window arrangement without an entrance.
description_view_3: The rear side of the building is visible, showing a plain wall with no windows or doors and the roof's overhang.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are symmetrically arranged in three horizontal rows per floor, with even spacing and alignment across the facade.
